hand_profile.script_style: Spanish notarial cursive, late 18th to early 19th-century, with highly regular, slanted, and flourished letterforms typical of formal legal documents.

letter_formations.u_v: u and v are visually distinguishable; 'u' is rounded and open at the bottom, 'v' is pointed.
letter_formations.f_s: f and s are distinguishable; 'f' has a crossbar and descender, 's' is long-s in medial positions and round-s at the end.
letter_formations.s: both long-s and round-s are present; long-s is used medially, round-s terminally.
letter_formations.a: single-compartment, open at the top, with a slight rightward flourish.
letter_formations.e: small, open loop, sometimes with a pronounced upper flourish.

hand_profile.marginalia_position: upper left margin and upper center margin
hand_profile.dropcap_description: Large, highly flourished 'E' at the beginning of the main text, occupying several lines in height.
special_challenges: Highly flourished capitals can obscure adjacent letters
Long descenders and ascenders overlap lines
curator_rules: u and v are used interchangeably
f and s are used interchangeably
ç old spelling is always modern z
accents are inconsistent — transcribe exactly as written
horizontal cap over letter means n follows
long-s and round-s both present — transcribe as written
abbreviation marks: transcribe the expansion not the symbol
